Compute AmsPAF for a single sample

Source: R/mspaf.R
compute_amspaf_one_sample.Rd

Thin wrapper over the shared AmsPAF helpers (.amspaf_adjust(), .amspaf_add_paf(), .amspaf_combine()) that processes one sample in isolation. Retained so the normalisation / ARA / CA / IA steps can be driven (and unit-tested) for a single sample; the batched compute_amspaf_per_sample() path uses the same helpers so behaviour is guaranteed identical.

Usage

compute_amspaf_one_sample(
  sample_rows,
  ref_table,
  ssd_params,
  min_analytes,
  method,
  guideline_dir,
  has_imputed = FALSE,
  ara_enabled = TRUE
)

Arguments

sample_rows

Long-format chemistry rows for one sample (one row per analyte; may include co-analyte rows used for normalisation). Must carry a detected column.

ref_table

Tibble (analyte, ref_norm) from prep_ref$ref_table.

ssd_params

Tibble from derive_ssd_params() (incl. the fit column).

min_analytes

Minimum analytes required.

method

SSD method (used only for the rare NULL-fit fallback).

guideline_dir

Path to ANZG XLSX folder (NULL-fit fallback only).

has_imputed

Logical; whether the input carried an imputed column (controls n_analytes_imputed accounting).

ara_enabled

Logical; whether the caller supplied a reference. Controls the ref_source diagnostic (see .amspaf_adjust()).

Value

A one-row tibble (or zero-row tibble if the sample fails min_analytes) with columns value, n_analytes_used, n_analytes_imputed, dominant_analyte, max_paf, analyte_pafs, dropped_analytes.
